landing.title: Fibery — Operating system for orgs run by nerds
landing.text:
Fibery — Operating system for orgs run by nerds
Solutions
Tailored for your business
Product
Turn customer insights into real products without breaking your flow.
Digital agency
Collaborate with clients, manage projects, and connect work to a built-in CRM.
Software development
Run sprints your way, manage tasks, and build a wiki that actually helps.
Startup
Invent, build, and grow — without drowning in tools you don’t need.
Looking for something different?
No-code
Design your perfect workspace from the ground up with no-code.
Add any template, any time
Sales CRM
OKR Tracking
Recruitment
View all templates →
Platform
Building blocks
Features
Explore our most powerful features that go with every plan.
Templates
Discover, install and fully customize any template in minutes.
Integrations
Get a single connected workspace where all teams are welcome.
Fibery AI
Brainstorm, automate tasks, find relevant data, and experiment with your process.

We didn’t plan this, but Fibery is a
good fit
god’s gift for AI agents
Operating system for orgs run by nerds
A modular work platform that replaces scattered tools and brings everything and everyone together.
Sign up
Book a Demo
Mo tools
mo problems
Notorious D.O.G.
Instead of every team and every kind of data having its own isolated tool…
Whiteboard
Pastures
Spreadsheet
Slums
Issue
Factories
Productivity
Obelisks
Document
Graveyards
…get everyone and everything together in one collaborative and interconnected workspace:
drag to explore
Startup
Enterprise
Agency
Non-profit
We're constantly surprised by the kind of orgs that run on Fibery: a self-driving boats startup, a Bible translating non-profit, a cannabis farm.
Everything is deeply
intertwingled
Ted Nelson
Fibery allows me to
break the silos between business processes
, either by replacing them with Fibery spaces or connecting them through integrations. This is an organizational quantum leap!
Mikael Klingvall
Chief Ontology Officer, Dairdux
Sure, we have templates
to get you started
Opportunity Solution Tree
JTBD
Atomic research
OKR Tracking
SPADE framework
Allocation Management
Retrospectives
Software Development
Content Marketing
Event Planning
Market research
Sales CRM
30-60-90 Onboarding
Employee Management
Hiring
Vacations
Finance
StartUp accelerator management
Project Management
Meeting Notes
Templates
But Fibery thrives when you
make it truly yours.
Deep inside, you’re
an architect, aren’t you?
Tell it how you want to run your team
— and Fibery will adapt.
Due to its modular nature it can handle whatever you throw at it.
Relational Databases
The Core
Views
The Face
Table
Board
Gallery
List
Timeline
Gantt
Calendar
Report
Map
Feed
Form
Dashboard
Hierarchy, as deep as you need
Copy-paste cells
If you excel at Excel, this will be easy
Multiple kinds of data
Swimlanes and color coding
Inline edit
Nothing to flex about
Hierarchy, as deep as you need
Inline edit
Multiple kinds of data
Actual vs. planned dates
Dependency-aware rescheduling
Collapsible hierarchy
Actual vs. planned dates
Dependency-aware rescheduling
Multiple kinds of data
Color coding
A freaking BI tool, a product inside product
Barcharts, scatterplots, tables, etc.
Based on actual or historical data
Color coding
Satellite view
Scrollable feed based on your data
Rich-text description
Required fields
Hidden pre-selected fields
Multiple Views on one screen
Docs & whiteboards
Embeds from other tools
Automations
The Machine
Formulas
The Math
Access Templates
The Keys
few building blocks to learn, infinite ways to put them together
Complex, not complicated
Features
This seems a lot
more powerful than Notion
. I'm extremely surprised this isn't extremely popular. Just wondering if I am missing something, like if the CEO tortures small kittens.
O
One dude
on Reddit
The explanation is simple: we are much better at product and engineering than at sales and marketing.
However, you are here, so it means that we are doing something right. Finally!
Oh, and also I waterboard kittens.
Michael,
Fibery
Powerful for those who can handle it,
Simple for everyone else
Teammates and volunteers don't have to know about the complexity - you can prepare familiar and simple Views for adding the data, working with that and etc.
C
Calvin G.
A kind human being
We have nuns using Fibery, for God's sake.
Architect Mode
Docs
The Swarm
Whiteboards
The Chaos
Smart Search
The Light
Mobile App
The Flash
When everything is connected,
AI finally makes sense
Since everything's in the same place and neatly organized in a relational database, AI can answer pretty challenging questions spanning multiple teams and processes:
Understanding > Productivity
Bring the data from the tools that you cherish into Fibery and connect with the rest of your workspace
Not ready to go all-in?
Integrations
Native connectors
No sweat
Integration platforms
Some sweat
Custom connectors
Real sweat
We won’t leave you alone
(unless you ask)
Polina, Chris, and Kseniya would be happy to support you in bringing any process (however weird) into Fibery.
Chat now
Schedule a call
The support team is super friendly, approachable and helpful even when it comes to assisting with complicated scenarios, solving problems, or suggestions - big ups to them!
Greg Bacchus
Chief Architect, Directed Technologies
We will annoy you
with (usually meaningful) product updates every Thursday
It's insane how fast you guys deliver & build.
Yvette Lans
CEO, Acceleright
I’m always blown away how
updates make sense
. It is well thought every time.
Comparing to my time with ClickUp this is next level s*it.
Jan Drabek
Developer, Better Marketing
In progress:
vibe-coded apps on top of Fibery, better performance on large datasets, suggestion mode in docs.
May 2026
configurable users visibility, more reliable MCP server, mixing lists in rich text.
April 2026
generate interfaces with AI, display fields from relations, developer portal.
March 2026
more capable MCP server, drag'n'drop for rich-text blocks, conditional entity views.
See changelog
The last work platform you'll switch to?
Get started
Schedule a demo
This is what I love about Fibery.
It grows with you
. As you learn new things, as you expand, as you change your workflow - Fibery is there, ready to fit your needs
Adam Fendrych
Product Manager, Outfindo
See what our customers say about Fibery — yes, even the ugly stuff
Operating system for orgs run by nerds
Rated 4.8 on
G2
and
Capterra

Support
User guide and AI support
Chat and email
support
Our team of solution architects are happy to advise you on how to map your workflows into Fibery and answer any questions you have.
Migration
assistance
We're always happy to suggest the easiest way to migrate knowledge and work from your existing tools into Fibery. For a bit of extra money, we can even take care of it for you—white gloves and all.
Chat with us
Chat with us
Chat with us
Customer success
manager
You’ll have a dedicated solution architect who understands the nuances of your workspace and provides tailored recommendations.
Billing
Option to pay by bank transfer
Available if you pay annually or the invoice amount exceeds $1000.
Multi-workspace
billing
Get a consolidated invoice and pay for each user once, regardless of how many workspaces they are in.
Custom contract
Questions & Answers
Is there a trial period?
Yes, every workspace gets a 14-day Pro plan trial. Fibery is powerful and takes time to explore. So you need time to procrastinate, receive some reminders, get into Fibery again, and, finally, "get" it. We think you can do it in 14 days. If you'd like to have a jump start, we suggest our
concierge support
.
What happens when the trial is over?
Once the trial is over, we'll ask you to make a decision: you can continue with Pro, select another paid plan, or switch to Free. No need to wait until the end of the trial period though: add billing details and select your plan at any point during the first 14 days to ensure uniterrupted experience.
Is there really no entities limit on the Free plan?
Yes, you can have any number of rows in your databases.  The performance depends on your usage patterns—for example, Tables handle large volumes of data better than Boards, and complex formulas take longer to calculate in large databases. If you have fewer than 50K entities, you'll almost certainly be fine. If you have more than 500K data points, consider aggregating them before adding to Fibery.
What about these 6 months for free for startups?
Get 6 months of Fibery for free if your company has fewer than 42 employees.
Ditch those scattered spreadsheets, and let’s grow together.
Learn more & apply
What happens if we invite/deactivate users when on a paid plan?
When you invite a new user, we charge you proportionally to the time left in the billing cycle. For example, if you pay annually and there are six months left until the next payment, the prorated amount is
$
12*(6/12)*12=
$
72.
When you deactivate a user, the unspent amount will be deducted from your next bill.
What payment methods do you accept?
We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and Apple Pay. Pro and Enterprise customers can also pay via bank transfer. Credit cards and Paypal are available via self-service at checkout. Please contact us for Apple Pay or bank transfer payments.
How is the payment being processed?
We use Chargebee at the checkout and Braintree to process your payment.
Braintree
handles billions of dollars in transactions and is trusted by companies like Uber, Dropbox, and GitHub. We do not store or handle your credit card information directly.
Do you offer refunds?
We do. If you've made a mistake while signing up for a paid subscription, accidentally added paid members to your workspace, or simply lost enthusiasm for Fibery, please reach out to us to us via chat or at
new@fibery.io
within 30 days of payment.
Can we cancel our paid plan?
Sure, feel free to cancel your subscription at any time. All

analysis.name: Fibery
analysis.tagline: Operating system for orgs run by nerds
analysis.value_proposition: A modular, no-code work platform that replaces scattered tools with one interconnected relational workspace for the entire company.
analysis.ideal_customer: Product, engineering, and cross-functional teams at startups, agencies, and mid-market orgs who want to build a custom connected workspace instead of stitching together SaaS tools.

features.name: relational databases
features.description: Core building block: custom databases with typed fields and cross-space relations.

features.name: table view
features.description: Spreadsheet-style view with hierarchy, inline edit, copy-paste, multiple data kinds.

features.name: board view
features.description: Kanban board with swimlanes and color coding.

features.name: list view
features.description: Hierarchical list view with inline edit.

features.name: calendar view
features.description: Calendar layout for date-based entities.

features.name: timeline view
features.description: Timeline with actual vs. planned dates and dependency-aware rescheduling.

features.name: gantt view
features.description: Gantt chart with dependencies and collapsible hierarchy.

features.name: gallery view
features.description: Card-based gallery layout.

features.name: feed view
features.description: Social-media-style scrollable feed for notes, briefs, or specs.
features.evidence_quote: Imagine a social media feed but for your notes, briefs, or specs.

features.name: map view
features.description: Satellite/map view of geo-tagged data.

features.name: form view
features.description: Forms for structured data intake with required and hidden pre-selected fields.
features.evidence_quote: Form — Required fields, Hidden pre-selected fields

features.name: dashboard view
features.description: Composable dashboards combining multiple views on one screen.

features.name: charts and reports
features.description: Built-in BI tool: bar charts, scatterplots, tables from live or historical data.
features.evidence_quote: Charts — We have a custom-made BI tool inside Fibery

features.name: historical data reporting
features.description: Charts include historical data on Standard and above.

features.name: docs
features.description: Collaborative rich-text documents inside the workspace.

features.name: whiteboards
features.description: Freeform whiteboards for brainstorming and diagramming.

features.name: smart folders
features.description: Dynamic folders that organize the sidebar around teams, objectives, or clients.
features.evidence_quote: Dynamic folders organizing the sidebar around your teams, objectives, or clients.

features.name: entity views
features.description: Customizable per-record layouts; rules to pick view based on entity state.
features.evidence_quote: customize how you see a database record when you open it. Some call it layouts.

features.name: view rules
features.description: Conditional entity view selection based on rules.

features.name: formulas
features.description: Spreadsheet-like formulas across databases and relations.

features.name: automations
features.description: No-code automations triggered by data changes.

features.name: js automations
features.description: JavaScript-based custom automations for advanced workflows.

features.name: unlimited automations
features.description: Removed automation caps on Enterprise plan.

features.name: integrations
features.description: Native connectors that pull external tool data into Fibery.

features.name: unlimited integrations
features.description: Removed integration caps on Enterprise plan.

features.name: custom connectors
features.description: Build custom integrations via developer APIs.

features.name: integration platforms
features.description: Support for iPaaS-style middle-layer platforms.

features.name: email integration
features.description: Email-in / email-to-entity ingestion.

features.name: fibery ai
features.description: Built-in AI that queries and acts across the relational workspace.
features.evidence_quote: Fibery AI — Brainstorm, automate tasks, find relevant data

features.name: ai included on all plans
features.description: AI capabilities bundled into every tier including Free.

features.name: fibery mcp server
features.description: MCP server exposing Fibery to AI agents like Claude/ChatGPT/Cursor.

features.name: highlights
features.description: AI/text highlights extracted from documents and feedback.

features.name: smart search
features.description: AI-powered cross-workspace search.

features.name: templates library
features.description: Prebuilt installable templates (CRM, OKR, sprints, JTBD, etc.).
features.evidence_quote: Discover, install and fully customize any template in minutes.

features.name: okr tracking template
features.description: OKR management template.

features.name: sales crm template
features.description: Prebuilt sales CRM template.

features.name: recruitment template
features.description: Hiring/recruitment template.

features.name: software development template
features.description: Sprints/tasks/wiki template for engineering teams.
features.evidence_quote: Run sprints your way, manage tasks, and build a wiki

features.name: opportunity solution tree template
features.description: Teresa Torres–style product discovery template.

features.name: jtbd template
features.description: Jobs-to-be-done research template.

features.name: atomic research template
features.description: Atomic user research template.

features.name: spade framework template
features.description: SPADE decision-making template.

features.name: retrospectives template
features.description: Team retro template.

features.name: content marketing template
features.description: Editorial/content planning template.

features.name: event planning template
features.description: Events management template.

features.name: market research template
features.description: Market research capture template.

features.name: 30-60-90 onboarding template
features.description: New-hire onboarding plan template.

features.name: employee management template
features.description: HR/employee tracking template.

features.name: hiring template
features.description: Hiring pipeline template.

features.name: vacations template
features.description: PTO/vacations tracker.

features.name: finance template
features.description: Finance/allocation tracking template.

features.name: allocation management template
features.description: Resource/capacity allocation template.

features.name: startup accelerator management template
features.description: Template for running an accelerator program.

features.name: project management template
features.description: Generic project management template.

features.name: meeting notes template
features.description: Meeting notes template.

features.name: observers
features.description: Free read-only users who can comment.
features.evidence_quote: Invite your colleagues as free, read-only users who can comment.

features.name: guests
features.description: Free per-entity collaboration for partners, clients, and freelancers.
features.evidence_quote: Collaborate with partners, clients, and freelancers on specific entities without having to pay for extra seats

features.name: user groups
features.description: Group-based user management for permissions.

features.name: advanced permissions
features.description: Granular access control.

features.name: access templates
features.description: Reusable access/permission templates.

features.name: public sharing
features.description: Share entities and views publicly.

features.name: version history
features.description: Undo and history for entities and docs.

features.name: undelete / history
features.description: Restore deleted entities from history.

features.name: unlimited version history
features.description: No cap on entity version retention on Pro+.

features.name: saml sso
features.description: SAML single sign-on for Enterprise.

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: SCIM-based user provisioning.

features.name: data residency
features.description: Choice of US, EU, UK, or APAC hosting region.
features.evidence_quote: US, EU, UK, or APAC data residency

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM on Enterprise plan.

features.name: human support
features.description: Live human support included on Standard+.

features.name: mobile app
features.description: Mobile app for on-the-go access.

features.name: desktop app
features.description: Downloadable desktop app.

features.name: browser extension
features.description: Browser extension for capturing to Fibery.

features.name: file storage
features.description: Attached-file storage; 1 GB on Free, unlimited on paid.

features.name: unlimited entities
features.description: No cap on records across any plan.

features.name: unlimited databases (paid)
features.description: Free capped at 10 databases; paid tiers uncapped.

features.name: cross-space relations
features.description: Native relations between databases across spaces.
features.evidence_quote: Fibery natively supports relations between databases from different Spaces

features.name: lookup fields
features.description: Lookup and rollup-style field types.
features.evidence_quote: more advanced ones like people, workflow, and lookup

features.name: workflow field
features.description: Built-in workflow/state field type.

features.name: architect mode
features.description: Schema-editing mode for building custom workspaces.

features.name: embeds
features.description: Embed content from other tools inside docs and views.

features.name: api
features.description: Public API for programmatic access.

features.name: public roadmap
features.description: Public roadmap visible to customers.

features.name: enterprise-level security
features.description: SOC2-caliber security posture marketed on landing.

differentiators: Modular no-code platform that replaces many tools with one connected workspace
Relational database core with cross-space relations
AI included on every plan (including Free) plus MCP server
Free observers and free guests on all plans (only Admins/Members are billed)
Built-in BI/charts tool ('a product inside product')
Free tier for teams up to 10 users, not just individuals
Data residency choice across US, EU, UK, APAC

recent_ships.what: Changelog updated June 25, 2026 (specific entries not accessible; changelog page 404)

praised: 'This seems a lot more powerful than Notion. I'm extremely surprised this isn't extremely popular.' — Reddit user quoted on landing
'Fibery allows me to break the silos between business processes… an organizational quantum leap!' — Mikael Klingvall, Dairdux
'Fibery costs almost as much as each of the four tools that we used previously.' — Nikita Fedoseev, enKod
Rated 4.8 on G2 and Capterra (per Fibery's own marketing)
complaints: Implied learning curve — marketed as 'Powerful for those who can handle it' suggesting complexity
Reliability incident acknowledged: '2.5 hours downtime post-mortem' HN post
Self-described weakness: 'we are much better at product and engineering than at sales and marketing'

free_tier.limits: up to 10 users + 10 guests, 10 databases, 1 GB file storage, 14-day version history, private-only advanced views

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: 10 users, 10 databases, unlimited entities, docs, formulas, automations, integrations, AI

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited DBs, AI, Charts, Whiteboards, human support; free observers & guests

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: More AI, user groups, advanced permissions, JS automations, unlimited version history

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited automations & integrations, SAML SSO, SCIM, data residency, CSM

pricing.notes: Only Admins/Members are billed; Observers and Guests are free on every tier. Yearly billing saves 20%. Enterprise has a 25 paid-user minimum. 50% discount for non-profits, educational, and Ukrainian orgs; 100% discount for open-source. Startup program: 6 months free on Pro (up to $6,048 value).
